meta.methode: Statische Analyse aus Java Class-Files (kein Quellcode vorhanden)

module_structure.Beschreibung: Produktkatalog, Kategorien, Preise, Varianten, Lager, Promotions, Lieferanten
Bestellverwaltung, Warenkorb, Checkout, Rückgaben, Angebote, Shopping-Listen
Proprietäres ORM: Delegator, GenericEntity, EntityQuery, Transaktionen, Kryptographie
Utilities (DateTime, HTTP, XML, Cache), Konfiguration, Container-Lifecycle, Konvertierungen
Service-Engine: Dispatching, Job-Scheduler, JMS-Integration, RMI, Service-Metadaten
Interpreter für proprietäre XML-DSL (MiniLang); seit 18.12 deprecated
Buchhaltung, Rechnungen, Zahlungen, Hauptbuch, Budgets, Anlagevermögen, 8 Payment-Provider
Deklarative UI-Engine: Screen-, Form-, Menu- und Tree-Renderer (FreeMarker/FO/Macro)
Content-Management, Datei-Verwaltung, Surveys, Blog, SFTP-Client, FTP-Integration
Servlet-Controller, Request-Routing, Login, Session, WebDAV, CSRF-Schutz, JWT
Admin-Werkzeuge: Cache-Verwaltung, DB-Tools, Service-Browser, Testausführung, FO-Druck
Stammdaten für Personen/Organisationen, Kontaktdaten, Kommunikation, Einladungen
E-Mail, Geo-Daten, Login-Services, Portal-Seiten, Einstellungen, QR-Code, Status
Fertigung: Stücklisten (BOM), Produktionsaufträge, Routing, MRP, Kostenkalkulation
Versand, Kommissionierung, Verpackung, Carrier-Integration (UPS, FedEx, DHL, USPS)
Aufgaben, Projekte, Kalender-Events, Ressourcenzuweisung, Arbeitszeiterfassung
Entity-Synchronisation zwischen Instanzen, Cache-Services, Daten-Import/Export, Upgrades
Strukturierte Dateiimporte (CSV, Fixlänge) über konfigurierbare Metadaten-Definitionen
Marketing-Kampagnen, Tracking-Codes, SFA-Leads und Account-Management
Berechtigungsprüfung, CSRF-Strategien; enthält auch ZUGFeRD/E-Rechnung (Mustang)
Basis-Testklassen für Services und Groovy-basierte Integrationstests
Tomcat-spezifische Klassen: SSO-Valve, HTTPS-Redirect, Servlet-Container-Hooks
System-Info-Services, Setup-Assistent für neue Instanzen
Erweiterte Login-Events, Zertifikat-Services (SSL-Client-Zertifikate)
Personalwesen: Mitarbeiterdaten und HR-Events (minimale Implementierung)
Sales Force Automation: Kontakt-Export via vCard (ezvcard)

language_split:
Von den 2.932 Class-Files stammen:
- **~70 % (≈2.050)** aus Java-Quellcode — sauber dekompilierbar
- **~30 % (≈880)** aus Groovy-Skripten — als `.class` vorhanden, Quellcode nicht rekonstruierbar

Die Groovy-Klassen sind Groovy-Skripte des Service-Frameworks (Business-Logik als Groovy-Scripts konfiguriert), erkennbar an `extends groovy.lang.Script` und Closure-Klassen (`$_run_closure`).

---
dependency_structure:
Kern-Framework (Basis aller Module):
  base ← entity ← service ← security
         ↓           ↓
       minilang    testtools

Business-Module (bauen aufeinander auf):
  accounting → entity, service, order, party, product, security
  order      → accounting, entity, service, party, product, marketing, content
  product    → entity, service, party, order, shipment, content
  party      → entity, service, accounting, product, content

Infrastruktur (querschnittlich):
  webapp   → base, entity, service, security, widget, minilang
  widget   → entity, service, security, minilang
  webtools → entity, service, webapp, testtools

sbom.Wichtigste Bibliotheken: Apache Commons (Lang3, IO, Collections4, CSV, Pool2, DBCP2), Guava, Jackson
Apache Axis2 (SOAP), Apache CXF (REST/JAX-RS), Axiom, WSDL4J, ROME
Apache Shiro, OWASP ESAPI, OWASP HTML Sanitizer, Auth0 JWT
Apache FOP (PDF), Apache POI (Excel), PDFBox, iText, Batik (SVG), Mustang (ZUGFeRD)

god_objects.Bewertung: Extremes God-Object — enthält Warenkorb, Pricing, Tax, Shipping, Payment-State

risks.Indikator: 5 javax.*-Pakete — Migration zu Jakarta EE erforderlich
~30 % der Logik nur als .class vorhanden, Quelltext verloren
Business-Module greifen direkt auf DB-Engine zu
Jeder Provider: eigene API, Update-Pflicht, PCI-DSS-Scope
DES-basierte LegacyStorageHandler noch aktiv als Fallback

missing_patterns.Implikation: Kein Spring, kein CDI — manuelle Objekt-Verdrahtung

minilang_risks.value: — vorliegender Code nutzt es noch aktiv
unkontrollierter Datenfluss aus externen Quellen in MiniLang-Ausdrücke kann Injection-Angriffe ermöglichen
MiniLang-Scripts liegen als XML-Konfiguration vor (nicht in Class-Files) — statische Analyse nicht möglich

replacement_assessment.Befund: Schwierig wegen Zyklen; Entity- und Service-Kern müssten zuerst extrahiert werden
~250 Endpunkte in 39 Events-Klassen — dokumentierbar und testbar
testtools-Modul vorhanden; Groovy-Testklassen für alle Module erkennbar
ShoppingCart (388 Methoden), OrderServices (84 Methoden) — zuletzt ablösen
Strangler-Fig-Pattern: schrittweise Ablösung über Events-Layer von außen nach innen

replacement_phases:
Phase 1 (Niedrigstes Risiko):
  → Shipment-Carrier-Integrationen (UPS/FedEx/DHL/USPS)
  → Payment-Gateway-Adapter (8 Provider, klar isoliert)
  → Reporting/Content-Module

Phase 2 (Mittleres Risiko):
  → Party-Modul (Stammdaten)
  → Product-Katalog-Funktionen
  → Manufacturing/WorkEffort

Phase 3 (Höchstes Risiko — zuletzt):
  → Order/ShoppingCart-Kern (God-Objects)
  → Entity-Engine (zentrale DB-Abstraktion)
  → Service-Engine (Framework-Kern)

sbom_libraries.description: Parser für Kommandozeilenargumente.
Kodierungs-/Dekodierungsroutinen (Base64, Hex, URL, Digest).
Erweiterte Collection-Typen und -Utilities (Multimap, Bag, BidiMap u.a.).
Lesen und Schreiben von CSV-Dateien nach RFC 4180.
JDBC-Connection-Pool auf Basis von Commons Pool 2.
Multipart-HTTP-Upload-Verarbeitung für Servlets.

vulns.summary: Apache Commons FileUpload, Apache Commons FileUpload: FileUpload DoS via part headers
vulns.details:
Allocation of resources for multipart headers with insufficient limits enabled a DoS vulnerability in Apache Commons FileUpload.

This issue affects Apache Commons FileUpload: from 1.0 before 1.6; from 2.0.0-M1 before 2.0.0-M4.

Users are recommended to upgrade to versions 1.6 or 2.0.0-M4, which fix

sbom_libraries.description: Lesen und Schreiben diverser Bildformate (PNG, TIFF, BMP, ICNS u.a.).
Hilfsmethoden für Datei- und Stream-Operationen (FileUtils, IOUtils).
Utility-Klassen für String-, Array-, Reflection- und Datumsoperationen.

vulns.summary: Apache Commons Lang is vulnerable to Uncontrolled Recursion when processing long inputs
vulns.details:
Uncontrolled Recursion vulnerability in Apache Commons Lang.

This issue affects Apache Commons Lang: Starting with commons-lang:commons-lang 2.0 to 2.6, and, from org.apache.commons:commons-lang3 3.0 before 3.18.0.

The methods ClassUtils.getClass(...) can throw StackOverflowError on very long inpu

sbom_libraries.description: Client-Implementierungen für FTP, SMTP, POP3, Telnet und weitere Protokolle.
Generisches Object-Pool-Framework (Basis für DBCP2).
Textverarbeitungs-Utilities (StringSubstitutor, Levenshtein, Escape-Funktionen).
Validierungsframework für E-Mail, URL, Kreditkartennummern u.a.
Erstellen, Signieren und Verifizieren von JSON Web Tokens (JWT/JWS).
Extrahiert EXIF-, IPTC- und XMP-Metadaten aus JPEG, PNG, RAW und weiteren Bildformaten.
Hochperformanter In-Process-Cache (Ersatz für Guava Cache) mit W-TinyLFU-Eviction.
Apache Tika Kern-API: erkennt MIME-Typen und extrahiert Metadaten aus Dateien.

vulns.summary: Apache Tika has XXE vulnerability
vulns.details:
Critical XXE in Apache Tika tika-core (1.13-3.2.1), tika-pdf-module (2.0.0-3.2.1) and tika-parsers (1.13-1.28.5) modules on all platforms allows an attacker to carry out XML External Entity injection via a crafted XFA file inside of a PDF. 

This CVE covers the same vulnerability as in CVE-2025-5498

sbom_libraries.description: Tika-Parser-Bundle für Office, PDF, Bilder, Audio, Video und weitere Formate.
Jakarta Mail 2.x: Senden und Empfangen von E-Mails über SMTP, IMAP, POP3.
Jakarta Mail 1.x (javax.*-Namespace, Legacy): wird für ältere Komponenten benötigt.
Apache POI: Lesen und Schreiben von Microsoft-Office-Formaten (XLS, DOC, PPT).
Apache POI OOXML-Erweiterung für XLSX, DOCX und PPTX (Open XML).

vulns.summary: Apache POI OOXML Vulnerable to Improper Input Validation in OOXML File Parsing
vulns.details: Improper Input Validation vulnerability in Apache POI. The issue affects the parsing of OOXML format files like xlsx, docx and pptx. These file formats are basically zip files and it is possible for malicious users to add zip entries with duplicate names (including the path) in the zip. In this case

Lineare Regex-Engine (Port von Google RE2) ohne Backtracking-Risiko.
HTML-Parser mit CSS-Selektor-API und eingebautem HTML-Sanitizer.
Apache HttpComponents Client 4.x für HTTP/HTTPS-Anfragen mit Verbindungspool.
De-/Serialisierung zwischen Java-Objekten und JSON (ObjectMapper).

vulns.details: The Socket Appender in Apache Log4j Core versions 2.0-beta9 through 2.25.2 does not perform TLS hostname verification of the peer certificate, even when the  [verifyHostName](https://logging.apache.org/log4j/2.x/manual/appenders/network.html#SslConfiguration-attr-verifyHostName)  configuration attri

sbom_libraries.description: iText 2.x (Legacy): PDF-Erstellung und -Manipulation (ältere API).

vulns.summary: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference in  iText
vulns.details: The XML parsers in iText before 5.5.12 and 7.x before 7.0.3 do not disable external entities, which might allow remote attackers to conduct XML external entity (XXE) attacks via a crafted PDF.

vulns.summary: Validator.isValidSafeHTML is being deprecated and will be deleted from org.owasp.esapi:esapi in 1 year
vulns.details:
### Impact
The `Validator.isValidSafeHTML` method can result in false negatives where it reports some input as safe (i.e., returns true), but really isn't, and using that same input as-is can in certain circumstances result in XSS vulnerabilities. Because this method cannot be fixed, it is being dep

sbom_libraries.description: Bereinigt HTML-Input nach OWASP-Richtlinien und entfernt XSS-Vektoren.

vulns.summary: OWASP Java HTML Sanitizer is vulnerable to XSS via noscript tag and improper style tag sanitization
vulns.details:
### Summary
It is observed that OWASP java html sanitizer is vulnerable to XSS if HtmlPolicyBuilder allows `noscript` and `style` tags with `allowTextIn` inside the style tag. This could lead to XSS if the payload is crafted in such a way that it does not sanitise the CSS and allows tags which is no

Observable Timing Discrepancy vulnerability in Apache Shiro.

This issue affects Apache Shiro: from 1.*, 2.* before 2.0.7.

Users are recommended to upgrade to version 2.0.7 or later, which fixes the issue.

Prior to Shiro 2.0.7, code paths for non-existent vs. existing users are different enough,

vulns.summary: Apache CXF is vulnerable to DoS attacks as entire files are read into memory and logged
vulns.details: Apache CXF stores large stream based messages as temporary files on the local filesystem. A bug was introduced which means that the entire temporary file is read into memory and then logged. An attacker might be able to exploit this to cause a denial of service attack by causing an out of memory exc

vulns.summary: Apache CXF: Denial of Service vulnerability with temporary files
vulns.details: A potential denial of service vulnerability is present in versions of Apache CXF before 3.5.10, 3.6.5 and 4.0.6. In some edge cases, the CachedOutputStream instances may not be closed and, if backed by temporary files, may fill up the file system (it applies to servers and clients).

Eingebetteter Apache Tomcat-Server (Servlet-Container, HTTP-Verarbeitung).

This issue affects Apache Tomcat: from 11.0.0-M14 through 11.0.20, from 10.1.22 through 10.1.53, from 9.0.92 through 9.0.116.

vulns.details:
Uncontrolled Resource Consumption vulnerability in Apache Tomcat if an HTTP/2 client did not acknowledge the initial settings frame that reduces the maximum permitted concurrent streams.

vulns.details:
Time-of-check Time-of-use (TOCTOU) Race Condition vulnerability in Apache Tomcat.

This issue affects Apache Tomcat: from 11.0.0-M1 through 11.0.1, from 10.1.0-M1 through 10.1.33, from 9.0.0.M1 through 9.0.97.

The mitigation for CVE-2024-50379 was incomplete.

vulns.details: Improper Input Validation vulnerability in Apache Tomcat. Incorrect error handling for some invalid HTTP priority headers resulted in incomplete clean-up of the failed request which created a memory leak. A large number of such requests could trigger an OutOfMemoryException resulting in a denial of

vulns.summary: Apache Tomcat installer for Windows has an untrusted search path vulnerability
vulns.details:
Untrusted Search Path vulnerability in Apache Tomcat installer for Windows. During installation, the Tomcat installer for Windows used icacls.exe without specifying a full path.

This issue affects Apache Tomcat: from 11.0.0-M1 through 11.0.7, from 10.1.0 through 10.1.41, from 9.0.23 through 9.0.105

Inconsistent Interpretation of HTTP Requests ('HTTP Request/Response Smuggling') vulnerability in Apache Tomcat via invalid chunk extension.

This issue affects Apache Tomcat: from 11.0.0-M1 through 11.0.18, from 10.1.0-M1 through 10.1.52, from 9.0.0.M1 through 9.0.115, from 8.5.0 through 8.5.100, f

This issue affects Apache Tomcat: from 11.0.0-M1 through 11.0.1, from 10

vulns.details:
Improper Input Validation vulnerability.

Improper Resource Shutdown or Release vulnerability in Apache Tomcat made Tomcat vulnerable to the made you reset attack.

This issue affects Apache Tomcat: from 11.0.0-M1 through 11.0.9, from 10.1.0-M1 through 10.1.43 and from 9.0.0.M1 through 9.0.107. Older, EOL versions may also be affected.

This issue affects Apache Tomcat: from 11.0.0-M1 through 11.0.7, from 10.1.0-M1 through 10.1.41, from 9.0.0.M1 through 9.0.105. The following versions were EOL at the time the CVE was created but are known to be af

vulns.summary: Apache Tomcat Vulnerable to Improper Resource Shutdown or Release
vulns.details: If an error occurred (including exceeding limits) during the processing of a multipart upload, temporary copies of the uploaded parts written to disc were not cleaned up immediately but left for the garbage collection process to delete. vulns.summary: Apache Tomcat has an Improper Input Validation vulnerability
vulns.details:
Improper Input Validation vulnerability in Apache Tomcat Native, Apache Tomcat.

vulns.summary: Apache Tomcat - Security constraint bypass with HTTP/0.9
vulns.details:
Improper Input Validation vulnerability in Apache Tomcat.

vulns.summary: Apache Tomcat Request and/or response mix-up
vulns.details:
Incorrect object re-cycling and re-use vulnerability in Apache Tomcat. Incorrect recycling of the request and response used by HTTP/2 requests could lead to request and/or response mix-up between users.

This issue affects Apache Tomcat: from 11.0.0-M23 through 11.0.0-M26, from 10.1.27 through 10.1.

vulns.summary: Apache Tomcat has an Improper Encoding or Escaping of Output vulnerability in the JsonAccessLogValve
vulns.details:
Improper Encoding or Escaping of Output vulnerability in the JsonAccessLogValve component of Apache Tomcat.

This issue affects Apache Tomcat: from 11.0.0-M1 through 11.0.20, from 10.1.0-M1 through 10.1.53, from 9.0.40 through 9.0.116.

Users are recommended to upgrade to version 11.0.21, 10.1.54 or

vulns.summary: Apache Tomcat Vulnerable to Improper Neutralization of Escape, Meta, or Control Sequences
vulns.details: Tomcat did not escape ANSI escape sequences in log messages. vulns.summary: Apache Tomcat - Security constraint bypass for pre/post-resources
vulns.details: Authentication Bypass Using an Alternate Path or Channel vulnerability in Apache Tomcat.  When using PreResources or PostResources mounted other than at the root of the web application, it was possible to access those resources via an unexpected path. vulns.summary: Apache Tomcat Vulnerable to Relative Path Traversal
vulns.details: The fix for bug 60013 introduced a regression where the rewritten URL was normalized before it was decoded. vulns.summary: Apache Tomcat Catalina is vulnerable to DoS attack through bypassing of size limits
vulns.details:
For some unlikely configurations of multipart upload, an Integer Overflow vulnerability in Apache Tomcat could lead to a DoS via bypassing of size limits.

This issue affects Apache Tomcat: from 11.0.0-M1 through 11.0.8, from 10.1.0-M1 through 10.1.42, from 9.0.0.M1 through 9.0.106. The following ve

This issue affects Apache Tomcat: from 11.0.0-M1 through 11.0.20, from 10.1.0-M1 through 10.1.53, from 9.0.13 through 9.0.116.

sbom_libraries.description: JSP-Compiler und -Runtime für den eingebetteten Tomcat.
Hochperformante JDBC-Connection-Pool-Implementierung von Tomcat.
Gruppen-Kommunikationsframework, das Tomcat-HA zugrunde liegt.
JDOM 1 (Legacy): ältere Java-XML-API, noch von einzelnen Komponenten verwendet.

vulns.summary: XML External Entity (XXE) Injection in JDOM
vulns.details: An XXE issue in SAXBuilder in JDOM through 2.0.6 allows attackers to cause a denial of service via a crafted HTTP request. As a workaround, to avoid external entities being expanded, one can call `builder.setExpandEntities(false)` and they won't be expanded.

sbom_libraries.description: JDOM 2: Einfache Java-API für DOM-ähnliche XML-Verarbeitung.
Apache Xerces2: validierender XML-Parser mit DOM- und SAX-Unterstützung.
XStream: Serialisierung von Java-Objekten nach XML und JSON ohne Annotationen.

vulns.summary: XStream is vulnerable to a Denial of Service attack due to stack overflow from a manipulated binary input stream
vulns.details:
### Impact
The vulnerability may allow a remote attacker to terminate the application with a stack overflow error resulting in a denial of service only by manipulating the processed input stream when XStream is configured to use the BinaryStreamDriver.

### Patches
XStream 1.4.21 detects the manipul

8 Libraries mit CRITICAL/HIGH-Schwachstellen: commons-fileupload, tika-core, itext. Siehe SBOM-Seite für Details.
Kein Spring, kein CDI — manuelle Objekt-Verdrahtung
5 javax.*-Pakete — Migration zu Jakarta EE erforderlich
~30 % der Logik nur als .class vorhanden, Quelltext verloren
Business-Module greifen direkt auf DB-Engine zu
Jeder Provider: eigene API, Update-Pflicht, PCI-DSS-Scope
DES-basierte LegacyStorageHandler noch aktiv als Fallback
Migration zu Jakarta EE (jakarta.*) erforderlich: javax.*, javax.servlet.*, javax.mail.*, javax.jms.*….
CFR-Dekompilierung ist bei 5 Java-Klassen fehlgeschlagen. Logik dieser Klassen ist nicht analysierbar.
Groovy-kompilierte Klassen liegen nur als Bytecode vor. Fachliche Logik dieser Scripts ist nur eingeschränkt analysierbar.
